title: Fermented, Creamy Uruguayan Beef Tagliata Pasta
description: A crowd-pleasing dairy-free pasta dish featuring fermented beef cutlets in a creamy sauce over al dente pasta.

ingredients.name: 1 lb (450g) penne or similar short pasta
2 small beef cutlets (about 6-8 oz/170-230g each, aged for at least 2 weeks)
1/4 cup (60ml) unsweetened almond milk or other non-dairy milk
Salt and black pepper, to taste
Pasta cooking water (for pasta coating), optional

instructions: Blanch the beef cutlets in boiling salted water until cooked through. Pat dry with paper towels.
In a saucepan, combine garlic, red wine vinegar, nutritional yeast, almond milk, and vegan butter. Stir to dissolve the nutritional yeast, then bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Add the blanched beef cutlets into the sauce and cook for about 2 minutes on each side or until heated through. Remove from heat.
Cook pasta in plenty of boiling salted water until al dente. Drain well and return to pot with a ladleful of pasta cooking water, if using.
Pour the creamy sauce over the cooked pasta, tossing gently so it adheres. Serve warm garnished with fresh parsley, if desired.
